content.data:
Now I have a clear picture. The plan:

1. **Data model**: Add optional `Sources` array to package object — `[{Source, ID}]` — for multi-source packages. Single-source packages stay as-is.
2. **Display**: Show `[S+W]` badge when both sources exist, otherwise existing `[S]`/`[W]` behavior.
3. **Install**: When a package has multiple sources, show a dialog asking which source to use.
4. **Add/Edit dialog**: Allow adding a second source+ID pair.
5. **JSON**: Merge existing `[S]`/`[W]` duplicate entries automatically on load.

input.__tool_use_purpose: Update New-PackageObject to support Sources array
input.newStr:
# Helper to create a standardized package object
# Sources: array of @{Source; ID} for multi-source packages. If null, uses single Source/ID.
function New-PackageObject {
    param($Name, $ID, $Source, $Category = "General", $Sources = $null)
    $sources = if ($Sources) { $Sources } else { @(@{Source=$Source; ID=$ID}) }
    $displaySource = ($sources | ForEach-Object { if ($_.Source -eq "scoop") { "[S]" } else { "[W]" } }) -join "+"
    return [PSCustomObject]@{
        Name          = [string]$Name
        ID            = [string]($sources[0].ID)
        Source        = [string]($sources[0].Source)
        Sources       = $sources
        SourceBadge   = $displaySource
        Category      = [string]$Category
        IsSelected    = $false
        RowBackground = [System.Windows.Media.Brushes]::Transparent
        Checkmark     = ""
    }
}

content.data:
Here's what changed:

**Multi-source merging on load** — existing `AutoHotkey v1 [S]` + `AutoHotkey v1 [W]` entries are automatically merged into a single item on next load. No manual JSON editing needed.

**Display** — the source badge now shows `[S]`, `[W]`, or `[S]+[W]` (in blue) instead of the raw source name.

**Add/Edit dialog** — now has two source rows: "Source 1 / ID" (required) and "Source 2 / ID" (optional, set to `(none)` to skip).

**Install / Uninstall** — when a package has both sources, a small popup appears asking **Scoop** or **Winget** before proceeding. Single-source packages install directly as before.

**Check Status** — checks all sources for a package; marks it installed if found in any of them.

Commit message: `feat: merge duplicate [S]/[W] packages into single multi-source items with source-selection on install`

content.data:
Found both bugs:

1. **Single-source items** (no `[S]`/`[W]` suffix) all strip to the same name and get merged into one entry — that's why you only see dual-source items.
2. **Source order** — not sorted, so winget/scoop order is random.

The fix: only merge items that actually have `[S]` or `[W]` in their name; leave plain-named items as-is. Also sort sources so winget always comes first.

0 errors. Both fixes are in:

- **Single-source items** (no `[S]`/`[W]` suffix) are now added directly — all your scoop/winget-only packages will show up again.
- **Source order** is always sorted winget-first, then scoop — both for legacy `[S]`/`[W]` merges and existing multi-source entries in JSON.
